canonical_definition: Based on the provided data, TOEFL11 is presented as a benchmark dataset used for research purposes. Formally named "The ETS Corpus of Non-Native Written English," its application is to operationalize and measure the task of Native language identification (NLI). The single paper surveyed uses TOEFL11 to conduct experiments on NLI. In this context, it is employed alongside at least one other dataset to evaluate performance on this task.
papers: CBP-Tuning: Efficient Local Customization for Black-box Large Language Models
KGECalibrator: An Efficient Probability Calibration Method of Knowledge Graph Embedding Models for Trustworthy Link Prediction
X-FLoRA: Cross-modal Federated Learning with Modality-expertLoRAfor MedicalVQA

definitions.text: The ETS Corpus of Non-Native Written English (TOEFL11) is a benchmark dataset used for Native Language Identification research.
definitions.paper: X-FLoRA: Cross-modal Federated Learning with Modality-expertLoRAfor MedicalVQA

relationships.paper: X-FLoRA: Cross-modal Federated Learning with Modality-expertLoRAfor MedicalVQA
relationships.evidence: We conduct experiments on two benchmark datasets for NLI: the ETS Corpus of Non-Native Written English (TOEFL11) (Blanchard et al., 2013) and Write & Improve Corpus 2024 (Nicholls et al., 2024). (Section 3)
